phases.name: Snapshot store, forward recorder, and dispatch wiring (backend persistence + recording pipeline)
phases.description: Stand up the DeliveryMetricSnapshot store as the single forward-recorded time-series source of truth, its EF repository, and the event-driven recorder that writes one row per delivery per day, dispatched exactly once after each portfolio forecast completes. Forward-only: no backfill, idempotent on (deliveryId, recordedAt.Date) via get-or-create overwrite, never a sentinel.

steps.name: DeliveryMetricSnapshot model, DbSet config, and EF migration across providers
criteria: DeliveryMetricSnapshot persists Id, DeliveryId, RecordedAt, TotalWork, DoneWork, RemainingWork plus nullable forward columns EstimatedTotalWork, ForecastHowMany, LikelihoodPercentage, WhenDistributionJson
FK DeliveryId to Delivery is ON DELETE CASCADE so snapshot rows never orphan when a delivery is deleted
A unique index on (DeliveryId, RecordedAt) backstops daily idempotency at the database
WhenDistributionJson uses the existing JSON value-converter idiom; no =true or CLR-initializer sentinels on persisted properties
The migration applies cleanly on a real Sqlite and Postgres provider, not just InMemory
steps.scenario_name: Deleting a delivery cascades away its snapshot rows
steps.implementation_notes: CREATE the model as a wide row; provision ALL nullable forward columns now so Slices 2-4 add no further migration. EXTEND LighthouseAppContext: DbSet<DeliveryMetricSnapshot>, FK DeliveryId->Delivery ON DELETE CASCADE (ADR-048), UNIQUE index on (DeliveryId, RecordedAt) (ADR-050), WhenDistributionJson value-converter mirroring the existing AdditionalFieldValues/StateMappings JSON-column converter. Generate the migration ONLY via Lighthouse.Backend/Create-Migration.ps1 -MigrationName <Name> (does sqlite + throwaway-postgres); NEVER dotnet ef migrations add. EF MIGRATION TRAP: a new column on a persisted model needs a real migration; InMemory tests stay green but real sqlite/postgres verify jobs FATAL on PendingModelChangesWarning at startup, so the migration-applies-on-real-provider check is mandatory (add regenerate-after-model-change to the pre-push checklist). NO =true / CLR sentinel on persisted props (sentinel non-idempotency + EF-migration lesson). nullable enabled, no dynamic, immutable record where viable. The cascade-delete AT mirrors DeliveriesControllerIntegrationTest / IntegrationTestBase. Tests: NUnit4002 Is.Zero not Is.EqualTo(0); CA1861 hoist constant array literals to private static readonly fields; no decorative/structural/provenance comments.

steps.name: IDeliveryMetricSnapshotRepository and EF adapter with get-or-create by (deliveryId, date)
criteria: IDeliveryMetricSnapshotRepository extends IRepository<DeliveryMetricSnapshot> and exposes get-or-create by (deliveryId, recordedAt.Date)
The EF adapter returns the existing row for a (deliveryId, date) pair instead of inserting a duplicate
Reads return snapshots for a delivery ordered by RecordedAt ascending
The repository is registered in DI alongside the existing repositories
steps.scenario_name: Re-handling the same day is idempotent on delivery and date
steps.implementation_notes: CREATE the port + EF adapter following the existing IRepository<T> + EF adapter pattern (RefreshLog/Delivery precedent). Get-or-create keys on recordedAt.Date (UTC date grain) so a same-day re-run overwrites in place; idempotency is structural (the unique index from 01-01 is the DB backstop), NOT a =true sentinel. Register in DI where sibling repositories are registered. CA1859: a private member returning an interface for a single concrete type should use the concrete type. Immutability: no mutating LINQ; order with .OrderBy, not in-place sort. Async all the way with CancellationToken; never .Result/.Wait(). The idempotency facet of the recorder handler test exercises this repo through the real EF context.

steps.name: PortfolioForecastsUpdated event and DeliveryMetricSnapshotRecordingHandler (the sole feed)
criteria: PortfolioForecastsUpdated(int PortfolioId) is a domain event record implementing IDomainEvent, mirroring PortfolioFeaturesRefreshed
The handler records each delivery's then-current TotalWork, DoneWork, and RemainingWork = TotalWork - DoneWork into today's row
Re-handling the same event the same day overwrites in place (idempotent on deliveryId and date), never duplicating rows
A re-opened item leaving Done lowers the next recorded day's DoneWork; forward columns are left null in Slice 1
The handler emits structured logging of duration, per-portfolio daily snapshot count, and exceptions
steps.scenario_name: Recorder upserts one row per delivery per day
steps.implementation_notes: CREATE the event as a record mirroring Models/Events/PortfolioFeaturesRefreshed.cs (the ONLY new event Epic 3993 introduces, ADR-049). CREATE the handler modeled on PortfolioFeaturesRefreshedMetricsInvalidationHandler: HandleAsync loads the portfolio's deliveries, projects each via DeliveryWithLikelihoodDto.FromDelivery (DRY of metric KNOWLEDGE), upserts today's row via the 01-02 get-or-create. Idempotency = get-or-create + overwrite-in-place, NOT a =true sentinel (forecast-minimum-data-guard lesson). Register in DI alongside the precedent handler. Structured logging per DELIVER action item 5 (duration + daily snapshot count + exception logging) so KPI-4 is inspectable. The recorder test sits beside PortfolioFeaturesRefreshedMetricsInvalidationHandlerTest / TeamDeletedRefreshLogCleanupHandlerTest and uses a real EF context via the test factory scope. FOLDED SCENARIO (no separate scenario_name slot — schema allows one per step): also un-Ignore + implement milestone-1 'A re-opened item lowers the next recorded done count' in the SAME test file — yesterday's recorder run records a higher DoneWork, an item leaves Done, today's run records a lower DoneWork with unchanged backlog. Tests: NUnit2056 prefer Assert.EnterMultipleScope; NUnit2045 independent asserts inside the multiple-scope; CA1861 hoist constant arrays. Records/with, early returns, max 2 nesting, no comments.

steps.name: Dispatch PortfolioForecastsUpdated once per completion on both update paths
criteria: PortfolioUpdater dispatches PortfolioForecastsUpdated AFTER UpdateForecastsForPortfolio and the forecast write-back, not the stale pre-forecast event
ForecastUpdater gains an IDomainEventDispatcher dependency and dispatches after its forecast write-back
The event is dispatched exactly once per portfolio-forecast-completion on each path (no missing dispatch, no double dispatch)
The recorded snapshot values are the fresh post-forecast figures, not the pre-forecast stale ones
steps.scenario_name: Recorder records fresh post-forecast figures, not stale
steps.implementation_notes: DELIVER action item 1 (dispatch-once invariant, high). EXTEND PortfolioUpdater.Update: dispatch via IDomainEventDispatcher.PublishAsync AFTER UpdateForecastsForPortfolio + forecast write-back (after ~line 84) — NOT the stale pre-forecast PortfolioFeaturesRefreshed at line 73. EXTEND ForecastUpdater.Update: it does NOT yet depend on IDomainEventDispatcher — ADD the constructor dependency, then dispatch after its UpdateForecastsForPortfolio + write-back. Exactly ONCE per portfolio-forecast-completion on EACH path: a missing dispatch is a silent KPI-4 gap, a double dispatch double-counts. FOLDED SCENARIO (no separate scenario_name slot): this step OWNS both integration-checkpoints scenarios — 'Recorder records fresh post-forecast figures, not stale' (the scenario_name) AND 'The forecasts-updated event is dispatched once per portfolio forecast completion' (both paths). A gated integration test asserts exactly one dispatch per completion on EACH of PortfolioUpdater.Update and ForecastUpdater.Update, and freshness (values are post-forecast, not the stale pre-forecast PortfolioFeaturesRefreshed figures). The pre-slice SPIKE (optional) confirms the exact dispatch lines. If the ctor crosses 7 params after adding the dispatcher, wrap the ctor declaration in #pragma warning disable/restore S107 (or an options object). S2325 make-static where applicable. Async all the way, CancellationToken passed through, no .Result/.Wait().

phases.name: metrics-history read endpoint (DTO + controller + read-API coverage)
phases.description: Expose ONE consolidated metrics-history endpoint that reads the snapshot store ordered by date, returns an honest empty series when no snapshots exist, and is gated by portfolio-read RBAC plus the inherited premium gate. Forward forecasting fields are null in Slice 1.

steps.name: DeliveryMetricsHistoryDto and point record (ADR-050 response shape)
criteria: DeliveryMetricsHistoryDto carries deliveryDate, nullable firstSnapshotDate, and a points array
Each point carries date, totalWork, doneWork, remainingWork plus nullable estimatedTotalWork, forecastHowMany, likelihoodPercentage, whenDistribution
The DTO projects from DeliveryMetricSnapshot rows with forward fields left null in Slice 1
Points are ordered by date ascending
steps.implementation_notes: CREATE the DTO + point record in API/DTO/ to the ADR-050 shape: { deliveryDate, firstSnapshotDate (nullable), points: [{ date, totalWork, doneWork, remainingWork, estimatedTotalWork?, forecastHowMany?, likelihoodPercentage?, whenDistribution? }] }. ONE endpoint carries every series (no separate /forecast endpoint, ADR-050); forward fields are null in Slice 1, populated by Slices 2-4. Use records, immutable construction, nullable enabled. The integration-checkpoints 'one endpoint carries every series' facet asserts the consolidated shape. CA1861 hoist constant arrays in tests.

steps.name: GET metrics-history endpoint, RBAC + premium gated, reads store
criteria: GET /api/v1/deliveries/{deliveryId:int}/metrics-history (and api/latest) returns the recorded series in date order
An empty store yields an honest empty points array, never an error or fabricated zeros
The endpoint carries [RbacGuard(PortfolioRead)] and a portfolio viewer can read the history
A non-premium instance does not expose the history (inherited premium gate)
steps.scenario_name: Endpoint returns the recorded backlog and done series in date order
steps.implementation_notes: EXTEND DeliveriesController (DELIVER may instead create a thin DeliveryMetricsController if controller cohesion warrants — open question, no architectural impact). Route GET /api/v1/deliveries/{deliveryId:int}/metrics-history + api/latest, [RbacGuard(PortfolioRead)], premium-gated by inheritance from the delivery surface. Read FROM the store ordered by date via the 01-02 repository; return empty points: [] honestly when no snapshots (D6 forward-only). RBAC: authorization flows through IRbacAdministrationService; no new write surface. CLIENTS follow-up (not a code step here): ONE new endpoint => version-gate obligation in the separate clients repo (FEATURE_REQUIRES_SERVER_NEWER_THAN, strictly newer than last released). The read AT mirrors CumulativeStateTimeReadApiIntegrationTest (WebApplicationFactory<Program> + EnsureDeleted/EnsureCreated + As* auth + Mock<ILicenseService>). Async all the way, early returns, no comments.

steps.name: Read-API coverage: empty store, RBAC viewer, premium gate, consolidated series
criteria: An empty store returns an empty series with a null firstSnapshotDate, not an error
A portfolio viewer (read access) can read the history; an unauthorized caller cannot
A non-premium instance is denied the history via the inherited premium gate
The consolidated response carries the backlog/done/remaining series with forward fields null
steps.scenario_name: Empty store yields an honest empty series, not an error
steps.implementation_notes: Un-Ignore and flesh out the remaining facets of the pre-scaffolded read-API AT (currently [Ignore("pending — DELIVER (delivery-metrics)")]): empty-store honest empty series (@error), AsPortfolioViewer read (@rbac — milestone-1 'A portfolio viewer can read the delivery metrics history'), non-premium denied via Mock<ILicenseService> returning false (@premium @error — milestone-1 'A non-premium instance does not expose...' AND integration-checkpoints 'The read is gated by premium and portfolio read access'), consolidated-series shape (@integration). WebApplicationFactory<Program> + real EF context + EnsureDeleted/EnsureCreated. NUnit: Is.Zero not Is.EqualTo(0) (NUnit4002); Assert.EnterMultipleScope (NUnit2056) with independent asserts (NUnit2045); CA1861 hoist any constant array literal to a private static readonly field; S3267/S6608 LINQ idioms. No decorative/structural/provenance comments in the test. Factory functions getMockX-style for fixture rows, validated against the production model; never redefine the schema in the test.

phases.name: Frontend Metrics tab, burnup chart, and E2E walking skeleton
phases.description: Surface the burnup in a new Metrics tab on the per-delivery DeliverySection accordion: a Zod-parsed fetch, a MUI-X burnup chart with backlog and done lines plus a forward-only empty state, lazily loaded on first tab open behind the inherited premium gate, proven end-to-end by a demo-data-seeded Playwright walking skeleton.

steps.name: Zod schema, fetch method, and DeliveryBurnupChart with Metrics tab
criteria: deliveryMetricsHistorySchema parses the endpoint response with forward fields nullable; the type is z.infer-derived
The fetch method requests metrics-history and parses the body through the schema
DeliveryBurnupChart renders a backlog line and a done line over a time axis with a delivery-date marker
The forward-only empty state reads 'builds forward from today — no snapshots recorded yet' when no snapshots exist
DeliverySection splits into a Work Items tab (existing grid) and a Metrics tab that lazily fetches on first open behind the premium gate
steps.scenario_name: Burnup renders backlog and done lines and the forward-only empty state
steps.implementation_notes: CREATE the Zod schema + z.infer type under src/models/Delivery/ with forward fields .nullable() (schema-first at the FE/BE trust boundary; the contract is probed by the schema, no Pact). EXTEND DeliveryService.ts with the GET parsed by the schema. CREATE DeliveryBurnupChart.tsx with MUI-X LineChart (reuse the StackedAreaChart area+line + scaleType:'time' idiom): backlog line + done line, delivery-date marker, forward-only empty state per D6. EXTEND DeliverySection.tsx: split AccordionDetails into a 'Work Items' tab (existing grid) + a 'Metrics' tab (charts), lazily fetch metrics-history on first Metrics-tab open, inherit canUsePremiumFeatures gate. FE Vitest+RTL test is DELIVER's to CREATE: renders backlog+done from a mocked endpoint response, and the forward-only empty state; assert BUSINESS behavior, not 'renders without crashing'. TS strict, never any (narrow unknown). S107 options-object when a function takes >3 params/any optional; S3776/S3358 extract axis-config helpers, no nested ternaries in JSX. UPPER_SNAKE_CASE module consts. Mock only abstractions you own (the DeliveryService), never fetch directly. No decorative/structural/provenance comments in prod or test.

steps.name: E2E walking skeleton: demo-snapshot seeding, spec, and POM
criteria: Demo-data seeding includes DeliveryMetricSnapshot rows so a fresh demo instance renders a populated burnup
A forecaster opens a delivery's Metrics tab and sees the backlog and done lines
The forecaster can switch back to the Work Items tab
The spec runs green against the locally started app with seeded demo data on a premium instance
steps.scenario_name: Forecaster opens a delivery's Metrics tab and sees the backlog and done lines
steps.implementation_notes: DELIVER action item 3 (demo-snapshot seeding, medium): a fresh demo instance is forward-only/empty, so EXTEND demo-data seeding with deterministic DeliveryMetricSnapshot rows (reuse the demo-data-time-in-state CSV-column precedent, MEMORY project_demo_data_time_in_state_via_csv_column) so the WS renders a POPULATED burnup — the single hard WS green-able pre-requisite. Un-fixme DeliveryMetrics.spec.ts (currently test.fixme) and flesh out the DeliveryMetricsTab POM. E2E uses DEMO data via loadDemoScenario, never live syncs; premium instance (license import recipe). ALL locators go through the POM (no inline page.locator in the spec); add a POM method when an element is needed. RUN the spec locally (dotnet run backend + license import + playwright) before committing — pnpm build only type-checks, not locator validity; do not punt to the user. Push implementation-invariant assertions into the backend integration / read-API tests, keep E2E minimal (one walking-skeleton scenario). Confirm the exact demo-seeding source location during DELIVER (DemoData services directory).

validation.notes: Reviewer flagged 3 folded-scenario traceability concerns; resolved by annotating implementation_notes (schema permits one scenario_name per step). 01-03 owns the re-open facet, 01-04 owns dispatch-once+freshness on both paths, 02-03 owns the premium+RBAC gate. All Slice-1 in-scope scenarios mapped; milestone-2/3/4/5 correctly excluded; deps form a clean DAG.
